title: Backup, Point-in-Time Recovery, Rebuild, and Disaster Reconciliation · Runtime Contract: Feed Go Backup Verification
summary: Backups exist but are untested, feed content is assumed refetchable, reader state shares no recovery objective, restore replays workers early, and corruption remains hidden. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go backup verification increment in rehearsed feed-service recovery system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Verify scheduled backups, WAL continuity, encryption, access, checksums, catalog, age, capacity, and restore eligibility.
Assign RPO, RTO, retention, encryption, restore, and rebuild strategy separately to accounts, subscriptions, reader state, feeds, entries, raw evidence, and telemetry.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Backup Verification · Theory Feed Go Backup Verification
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 1388 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove data-class objectives, backup eligibility, isolated restore, safe resume order, and disaster drills; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
